_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 1-1 General Pre-Hospital Care (Revised 05/08/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan General Treatment Protocol Section 1-1: General Pre-Hospital Care — MDHHS Approved 5/8/23, Reviewed 2023

sections.title: General Pre-Hospital Care
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 1-1: General Pre-Hospital Care — Applies to EVERY patient contact
points: Patient care should be initiated at the patient's side prior to patient movement or transport for most medical conditions — EVERY PATIENT CONTACT BEGINS WITH THIS PROTOCOL
Pediatric patients (< 14 years of age or up to 36 kg) are treated under pediatric protocols when applicable; refer to MI MEDIC cards for medication dosing and equipment sizes
Assess scene safety and use appropriate personal protective equipment
For trauma, refer to General Trauma-Treatment Protocol
A patient exhibiting any signs of a life-threatening illness or injury shall not be required to move on their own — includes patients with illnesses of unknown etiology
If applicable, refer to Adult or Pediatric Crashing Patient/Impending Arrest-Treatment Protocol
When indicated, implement airway intervention per the Airway Management-Procedure Protocol
When indicated, administer oxygen and assist ventilations per the Oxygen Administration-Procedure Protocol
Assess and treat other life-threatening conditions per appropriate protocol

Obtain vital signs including pulse oximetry if available or required, approximately every 15 minutes or more frequently as necessary (minimum 2 sets required for all patient transports; 2 sets suggested for refusals and treat-and-release patients)
Perform a secondary survey consistent with patient condition
Follow specific protocol for patient condition
Document patient care according to the Documentation and Patient Care Records Protocol
Establish vascular access per Vascular Access & IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol when fluid or medication administration may be necessary
Apply cardiac monitor and treat rhythm according to appropriate protocol

If applicable, obtain 12-lead ECG (per MCA selection — may be a BLS or Specialist procedure) per 12 Lead ECG-Procedure Protocol; provide copy of rhythm strip or 12-lead ECG to receiving facility with patient identifiers on strip
Use capnography/capnometry as directed per End Tidal Carbon Dioxide Monitoring-Procedure Protocol
NOTE: When possible, provide a list of the patient's medications or bring the medications to the hospital

mca_selections_required.description: May be designated as a BLS skill or Specialist (AEMT/Paramedic) skill

deterioration_flags: Any signs of life-threatening illness or injury — do not require patient to self-mobilize; initiate care at patient's side
Refer to Crashing Patient/Impending Arrest protocol if patient is deteriorating rapidly
scope_notes: 12-Lead ECG: scope per MCA selection — BLS or Specialist
Vascular access: Specialist (AEMT/Paramedic) skill unless MCA-approved for lower level
Cardiac monitor: Specialist skill unless MCA-approved
